content.text: Data Contract Schema Standards for AI Pipelines

A data contract is a formal agreement between a data producer and one or more consumers that specifies the schema, quality guarantees, operational SLAs, and governance terms for a data product. In AI pipelines, contracts serve a dual purpose: they prevent upstream schema changes from silently breaking retrieval or embedding pipelines, and they create an auditable record of what quality and governance properties each consumer was promised and can rely on.

attrs.alt: Data contract structure for AI pipelines

content.text: Why AI Pipelines Need Data Contracts
If a producer renames the field the ingestion pipeline reads as source text (e.g.
), every new chunk is embedded from an empty string. There is no error — the embeddings are just useless.
, a producer may stop populating them after a schema refactor. The retriever then serves chunks with no access control.
A consumer building a real-time compliance assistant needs data within 4 hours. A producer who thinks daily is fine violates the SLA without knowing it exists.
If the contract specifies embeddings from
without versioning, the consumer's retriever scores results against a different vector space.

id: dp-rag-policy-corpus-v2
version: 2.1.0
status: active          # draft | active | deprecated | retired
effective_date: "2024-01-15"
producer:
  team: data-platform
  contact: data-platform@example.com
  slack_channel: "#data-platform-support"
consumers:
  - team: rag-platform
    contact: rag-team@example.com
    use_case: "RAG retrieval for compliance assistant"
  - team: fine-tune-pipeline
    contact: ml-ops@example.com
    use_case: "Policy corpus fine-tuning dataset"
description: >
  Policy and regulatory document corpus delivered as ingestion-ready
  chunks with governance metadata for the RAG platform.

schema:
  format: json-schema
  version: "2020-12"
  fields:
    - name: doc_id
      type: string
      description: Stable document identifier (UUID)
      required: true
      nullable: false
    - name: chunk_id
      type: string
      description: Stable chunk identifier (UUID)
      required: true
      nullable: false
    - name: content
      type: string
      description: Chunk text (UTF-8, stripped of control chars)
      required: true
      nullable: false
      constraints:
        min_length: 50
        max_length: 2000
    - name: classification_label
      type: string
      description: Data classification (public | internal | confidential | restricted)
      required: true
      nullable: false
      allowed_values: ["public", "internal", "confidential", "restricted"]
    - name: permitted_apps
      type: array
      items: {type: string}
      description: App IDs permitted to retrieve this chunk
      required: true
      nullable: false
    - name: source_updated_at
      type: string
      format: date-time
      description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of source document update
      required: true
      nullable: false
    - name: content_class
      type: string
      description: Content classification per reference taxonomy
      required: true
      allowed_values: ["regulatory", "policy", "product_doc", "research", "market_data"]

# AI-specific schema extensions
ai_extensions:
  embedding_model: "text-embedding-3-small"
  embedding_dimensions: 1536
  chunk_strategy: "sliding_window"
  chunk_size_tokens: 512
  chunk_overlap_tokens: 64
  prompt_template_id: "rag-v2"   # which prompt template this corpus is designed for

quality_slas:
  completeness:
    description: Fraction of required fields populated
    threshold: 0.99        # ≥ 99% of chunks must have all required fields
    measurement: per_batch
  freshness:
    description: Max age of source_updated_at relative to ingestion time
    threshold_hours: 24
    measurement: p95       # 95th-percentile lag ≤ 24 h
  null_rate:
    description: Fraction of non-nullable fields that are null
    threshold: 0.01        # ≤ 1% null rate across all non-nullable fields
  duplicate_rate:
    description: Fraction of chunk_ids that appear more than once in a batch
    threshold: 0.0         # zero duplicates
  char_coverage:
    description: Fraction of source document characters represented in chunks
    threshold: 0.80        # ≥ 80% character coverage
  classification_validity:
    description: Fraction of classification_label values in the allowed_values set
    threshold: 1.0         # 100% valid labels required

operational_slas:
  ingestion_latency:
    description: Time from source document update to chunk available in RAG index
    p95_hours: 4
    p99_hours: 8
  breach_notification:
    description: Time to notify consumers of an SLA breach
    max_minutes: 15
    channel: "#data-platform-incidents"
  planned_downtime_notice:
    description: Notice period for planned schema changes or maintenance
    min_days: 5
  incident_response:
    - severity: P1          # retrieval completely broken
      response_minutes: 15
      resolution_hours: 1
    - severity: P2          # quality SLA breach
      response_hours: 1
      resolution_hours: 8
Consumers reading old name get no data
Consumer validation breaks on removed value
Consumers get better data; no code change
Treat embedding_model as a breaking change.
vectors without re-indexing the entire corpus. Increment MAJOR, notify all consumers, and coordinate a migration window.

Contracts are stored as YAML files in a contracts repository, versioned in git. A contracts registry (a simple API over the repo) allows consumers to discover available contracts, subscribe to change notifications, and check the current active version for a given data product.
All contract changes go through a PR with required reviews from producer and at least one consumer team lead.
When a contract is merged, the registry sends a notification to all registered consumer contacts listed in the
A CI check validates the YAML against the contract schema and runs a compatibility check against the previous version: MAJOR changes require a migration plan document linked in the PR.

Data contracts for AI pipelines extend standard data product contracts with AI-specific terms: the embedding model and dimensionality, chunking strategy, prompt template compatibility, and governance metadata requirements. Schema versioning with semver — where embedding model changes are MAJOR bumps — prevents vector space mismatches. Contract validation at ingestion time enforces quality SLAs before any chunk reaches the retriever. Publishing contracts in a versioned repository with PR review and consumer notifications ensures that producers and consumers share a single, auditable source of truth for every data dependency in the pipeline.
